volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 2.3.365b - Section 3.1.420)

sections.label: Section 2.3.365b
sections.original: Rogitas ? Summa forma semper conservam domi
sections.translation: Do you ask ? He will always see at home a fellow-servant of consummate beauty , and be conversing with her ; he will be in the same house with her ; sometimes he will take his meals with her ; sometimes sleep near her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.368
sections.original: Quid si nunc tute fortunatus fias ?
sections.translation: What now , if you yourself were to be this fortunate person ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.368b
sections.translation: By what means , Parmeno ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.369b
sections.translation: Do you assume his dress .

sections.label: Section 2.3.369c
sections.translation: His dress ! Well , what then ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.370
sections.translation: I ’ ll take you there instead of him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.370b
sections.translation: I hear you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.370c
sections.translation: I ’ ll say that you are he .

sections.label: Section 2.3.370d
sections.translation: I understand you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.371
sections.original: Tu illis fruare commodis quibus tu illum dicebas modo ;
sections.translation: You may enjoy those advantages which you just now said lie would enjoy ; you may take your meals together with her , be in company with her , touch her , dally with her , and sleep by her side ; as not one of these women is acquainted with you , nor yet knows who you are . Besides , you are of an age and figure that you may easily pass for a eunuch .

sections.label: Section 2.3.375
sections.original: Dixti pulchre : numquam vidi melius consilium dari .
sections.translation: You speak to the purpose ; I never knew better counsel given . Well , let ’ s go in at once ; dress me up , take me away , lead me to her , as fast as you can .

sections.label: Section 2.3.377
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Really , I was only joking .

sections.label: Section 2.3.377b
sections.translation: You talk nonsense .

sections.label: Section 2.3.377c
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! Wretch that I am ! what have I done ? ( CHAEREA pushes him along .) Whither are you pushing me ? You ’ ll throw me down presently . I entreat you , be quiet .

sections.label: Section 2.3.379
sections.translation: Let ’ s be off .

sections.label: Section 2.3.379b
sections.translation: Do you still persist ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.379c
sections.translation: I am resolved upon it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.379d
sections.original: Vide ne nimium calidum hoc sit modo .
sections.translation: Only take care that this isn ’ t too rash a project .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.translation: Certainly it isn ’ t ; let me alone for that .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380b
sections.original: At enim istaec in me cudetur faba .
sections.translation: Aye , but I shall have to pay the penalty [*] for this ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.380c
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 2.3.381
sections.translation: We shall be guilty of a disgraceful action .

sections.label: Section 2.3.381b
sections.original: An id flagitium est , si in domum meretriciam
sections.translation: What , is it disgraceful [*] to be taken to the house of a Courtesan , and to return the compliment upon those tormentors who treat us and our youthful age so scornfully , and who are always tormenting us in every way ;— to dupe them just as we are duped by them ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.385
sections.original: An potius haec patri aequum est fieri ut a me ludatur dolis :
sections.translation: Or is it right and proper that in preference my father should be wheedled out of his money by deceitful pretexts ? Those who knew of this would blame me ; while all would think the other a meritorious act .

sections.label: Section 2.3.387
sections.original: Quid istic ? si certum est facere facias : verum ne post conferas
sections.translation: What ’ s to be done in such case ? If you are determined to do it , you must do it : but don ’ t you by-and-by be throwing the blame upon me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.388b
sections.translation: I shall not do so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.388c
sections.translation: Do you order me , then ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.388d
sections.translation: I order , charge , and command you ; I will never disavow my authorizing you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.389b
sections.translation: Follow me ; may the Gods prosper it !

sections.label: Section 3.1.390
sections.original: Magnas vero agere gratias Thais mihi ?
sections.translation: Did Thais really return me many thanks ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.391
sections.translation: Exceeding thanks .

sections.label: Section 3.1.391b
sections.translation: Was she delighted , say you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.391c
sections.translation: Not so much , indeed , at the present itself , as because it was given by you ; really , in right earnest , she does exult at that .

sections.label: Section 3.1.393b
sections.original: Huc proviso ut , ubi tempus siet ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve come here to be on the look-out , that when there is an opportunity I may take the presents . But see , here ’ s the Captain .

sections.label: Section 3.1.394b
sections.translation: Undoubtedly it is the case with me

sections.label: Section 3.1.395
sections.original: Profecto ut grata mihi sint quae facio omnia .
sections.translation: , that every thing I do is a cause for thankfulness .

sections.label: Section 3.1.396
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ ve observed it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.396b
sections.translation: The most mighty King , [*] even , always used to give me especial thanks for whatever I did ; but not so to others .

sections.label: Section 3.1.398
sections.translation: He who has the wit that you have , often by his words appropriates to himself the glory that has been achieved by the labor of others .

sections.label: Section 3.1.400b
sections.translation: You ’ ve just hit it . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.400c
sections.translation: The king , then , kept you in his eye . [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.400d
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 3.1.401
sections.translation: To enjoy your society .

sections.label: Section 3.1.401b
sections.translation: True ; he intrusted to me all his army , all his state secrets .

sections.label: Section 3.1.402b
sections.translation: Astonishing !

sections.label: Section 3.1.402c
sections.translation: Then if , on any occasion , a surfeit of society , or a dislike of business , came upon him , when he was desirous to take some recreation ; just as though — you understand ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.1.404b
sections.translation: I know ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.405
sections.original: Quasi ubi illam exspueret iniseriam ex animo .
sections.translation: just as though on occasion he would rid his mind of those anxieties .

sections.label: Section 3.1.405b
sections.translation: You have it . Then he used to take me aside as his only boon companion .

sections.label: Section 3.1.406b
sections.translation: Whew ! You are telling of a King of refined taste .

sections.label: Section 3.1.407b
sections.translation: Aye , he is a person of that sort ; a man of but very few acquaintanceships .

sections.label: Section 3.1.408b
sections.translation: Indeed , of none , [*] I fancy , if he ’ s on intimate terms with you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.409b
sections.translation: All the people envied me ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.410
sections.original: Mordere clanculum : ego non flocci pendere .
sections.translation: and attacked me privately . I don ’ t care one straw . They envied me dreadfully ; but one in particular , whom the King had appointed over the Indian elephants . [*] Once , when he became particularly troublesome , Prithee , Strato , said I , are you so fierce because you hold command over the wild beasts ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.415
sections.original: Pulchre mehercle dictum , et sapienter . Papae !
sections.translation: Cleverly said , upon my faith , and shrewdly . Astounding ! You did give the fellow a home thrust . What said he ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.416b
sections.translation: Dumfounded , instantaneously .

sections.label: Section 3.1.417
sections.translation: How could he be otherwise ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.417b
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by our trust in you ! a lost and miserable fellow the one , and the other a scoundrel .

sections.label: Section 3.1.418b
sections.translation: Well then , about that : matter , Gnatho , the way in which I touched up the Rhodian at a banquet —

sections.label: Section 3.1.420
sections.translation: did I never tell you ?
